member.name: Helen Maguire
member.synopsis: Helen Maguire is the Liberal Democrat MP for Epsom and Ewell , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Primary Care and Cancer).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the UK’s current regulatory bodies in mitigating the harms associated with gambling advertisements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All gambling operators in the UK must comply with robust advertising codes, which are enforced by the Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) independently of Government. These codes apply across all advertising platforms, including broadcast, online and so...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of gambling advertisements on children and young people; and whether she plans to introduce further restrictions on advertising.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All gambling operators in the UK must comply with robust advertising codes, which are enforced by the Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) independently of Government. These codes apply across all advertising platforms, including broadcast, online and so...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he has taken to deliver breast screening for all women at increased risk through the NHS Breast Screening Programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Health Service breast screening for women at Very High Risk (VHR) starts earlier and is more frequent than those not at VHR. Guidance on eligibility for VHR screening is available at the following link:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the level of dependency on overseas AI providers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK recognises the risks that come with highly concentrated markets and the need to strengthen resilience in critical technologies. We see this less as an issue of dependence on any single country, but rather an overreliance on narrow supply chains or ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Disinformation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to help reduce disinformation from AI generated deepfakes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government is taking action to tackle harms caused by AI-generated content. Under the Online Safety Act (OSA), platforms must assess and mitigate the risk of illegal content, including where it is AI-generated, and take appropriate action to remove it.Gov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Education: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of VAT on independent school fees on state school capacity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury published a Tax Information and Impact Note (TIIN) on applying VAT to independent school fees, which includes the government’s estimations of the number of pupils expected to enter the state sector as a result of this policy. The TIIN can be a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of expanding the National Inherited Cancer Predisposition Register to include all women at increased risk of breast cancer.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving outcomes for people with breast cancer, including those at higher risk of developing the disease. The new and world-leading NHS National Inherited Cancer Predisposition Registry (NICPR), which is part of the Nation...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Women's Health Hubs: Breast Cancer
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that information about breast cancer and the importance of breast-checking is available in all women’s health hubs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Cancer Plan, published on 4 February this year, commits to improving public awareness of cancer, including breast cancer, increasing breast screening uptake, and supporting local awareness and early diagnosis initiatives through Cancer Allian...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support the testing and expansion of alternative pathways into breast clinics and ensuring that younger women can utilise these.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ensuring that people with breast symptoms have timely access to diagnostic services and clinics is a priority for the Government. Through the National Cancer Plan, we are rolling out breast pain clinics nationally by the end of 2026, helping provide alter...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Defending Democracy Taskforce
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness and impact of the Defending Democracy Taskforce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The work done by the Defending Democracy Taskforce and its members is vital in tackling threats to our democracy. Parliament is regularly updated about the progress and priorities of the Defending Democracy Taskforce, most recently in March 2026 when the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Early Retirement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what data his Department holds on the number of General Practitioners leaving the NHS before retirement age in each year since 2015, by numbers leaving more than 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40 and 4
writtenQuestions.answerText: Retirement age is not a fixed date and can vary depending on pension arrangements, the state pension age, and individual choices. Data held by the Department cannot therefore show how many years a general practitioner (GP) has left until retirement.NHS En...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps his Department is taking to ensure social media platforms are safe by design and are responsible for preventing harm, via the exposure of vulnerable adults and children, to r
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 15 June, the government published a progress statement announcing a ban on social media services from providing their services to under 16s, alongside targeted functionality restrictions on a wider range of online services, including a default on restr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, in what way he plans to ensure that women and girls are protected from AI harms on social media sites.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting women and girls online, including from AI-enabled harms, is a top priority for this Government as we work to halve violence against women and girls in a decade. The Online Safety Act places clear legal duties on platforms to proactively tackle ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, in what way he plans to ensure that social media companies are held accountable for the design of their platforms.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Online Safety Act places legal duties on platforms to assess and mitigate risks arising from the design and operation of their services, including how features and functionalities may facilitate harm. Companies must implement proportionate systems and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Espionage: China
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential national security implications of the convictions and sentencing of Bill Yuen and Peter Wai.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Upholding national security and keeping the public safe is the first duty of Government.This case proves the strength of the powers we have to protect us from hostile activity carried out by foreign states and the convictions should send a clear message –...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of whether the Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office in London continues to operate solely for trade and economic purposes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 21 May in response to Question 724.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Hong Kong: Oppression
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the role of Hong Kong authorities in acts of transnational repression directed at individuals resident in the United Kingdom.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 17 March in response to question 118999.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Nurses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how the Government plans to ensure there are sufficient metastatic breast cancer nurses to meet the commitment in the National Cancer Plan to provide every patient with a clinical nurse.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The metastatic breast cancer audit completed in 2025 highlighted several areas where the collection of data could be improved, and we are acting on these.The Government has set out in the National Cancer Plan for England an ambition to define and count re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Databases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps the government is taking to help ensure NHS Trusts are recording metastatic breast cancer data consistently and accurately.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The metastatic breast cancer audit completed in 2025 highlighted several areas where the collection of data could be improved, and we are acting on these.The Government has set out in the National Cancer Plan for England an ambition to define and count re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Patients
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that patient experience findings are published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The findings from a range of patient experience surveys undertaken or commissioned by NHS England and the Care Quality Commission are published nationally, including the GP Patient Survey, the Adult Inpatient Survey, the National Cancer Patient Experience...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
